input.headline: So long, plastic wet wipes - but should we be flushing the new ones?

raw.headline: So long, plastic wet wipes - but should we be flushing the new ones?

winner: New flushable wipes come with a warning label: 'Do NOT use these like toilet paper.' Apparently, they're more interested in our plumbing than our butts.

candidates.text: I asked the plumber if I could flush my new eco-friendly wipes and he said, "Sure, just make sure you don't have any regrets when the toilet starts filing a formal environmental impact report.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative twist with the toilet filing a report, good specificity and surprise, concise and natural language."}

candidates.text: I tried using biodegradable wet wipes for my plumbing repair, but then I remembered they’re not supposed to go down there—literally or legally!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Smart wordplay on 'literally or legally,' concise and specific with a clear setup and punchline."}

candidates.text: I tried using biodegradable wipes, but my toilet still didn't appreciate them — apparently, "flushable" isn't a promise, it's a dare.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Funny, concise, and uses a clever twist on the meaning of 'flushable' with a relatable scenario."}

candidates.text: So long, plastic wet wipes — but should we be flushing the new ones? Turns out, they’re biodegradable… in a century or two.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on biodegradability timeline, concise and specific with a touch of dry humor."}

candidates.text: So long, plastic wet wipes - but should we be flushing the new ones? "Looks like our bidets are getting a promotion — from toilet paper to wipe-wiping.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific, with a clever play on 'bidets getting a promotion' that ties back to the headline in a humorous way."}

candidates.text: New flushable wipes come with a warning label: 'Do NOT use these like toilet paper.' Apparently, they're more interested in our plumbing than our butts.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the warning label, specific to flushable wipes, concise and funny with a clever comparison."}

candidates.text: The government says we can stop flushing our wet wipes, but now we have to start apologizing to the toilet every time we use a new one.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with personification of the toilet, concise and specific to the headline topic."}

candidates.text: Why did the toilet paper decide to break up with its wet wipe partner? It said, "You're too flushed with yourself!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'flushed' and 'flushed with yourself,' but it's a bit forced and lacks specificity in connecting to the headline's context about new wipes."}

candidates.text: I guess we need to be careful with the new wipes because if we flush them, we're just taking things to the next wipe level.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the play on 'next wipe level,' but it's a bit forced and lacks specificity. It doesn't fully engage with the headline's context or deliver a strong punchline."}
